student_message: We would draw the fold line straight down the middle of the big square, from top to bottom!
final_message:
You are so smart! Drawing the fold line straight down the middle is exactly right. 🌟 Since you are amazing at making symmetrical designs, let's explore a super cool real-world example called block printing! 

This is a beautiful traditional craft from Rajasthan. Artisans carve a design into a wooden block and press it onto fabric over and over to make amazing repeating patterns. By stamping the block in different ways, the final design can have reflection symmetry, rotational symmetry, or both! The coolest part is that the print on the fabric is actually a mirror image of the carved block, because the block is pressed face-down onto the cloth. 

If you carved your beautiful square and triangle design into a wooden block, do you think the stamped picture would look exactly the same as the block? 🎨

orchestrator_intent.goal: Introduce symmetry in block printing patterns.
orchestrator_intent.reasoning: The section is not_started, so the dialogue agent will introduce the new concept building on the student's observation about the fold line.

current_section_content: Block printing is a traditional craft from Rajasthan where artisans carve a design into a wooden block and press it repeatedly onto fabric to create beautiful repeating patterns. When the same block is stamped multiple times in different orientations, the final printed design can have rotational symmetry, reflection symmetry, or both. Importantly, the print produced by a block is the mirror image of the carved block, because the block is pressed face-down. By analysing how a block is repeated and rotated, you can predict and identify the symmetry of the resulting pattern.
session_summary: The session covered lines of symmetry, rotational symmetry, and symmetrical designs. The student understands these well, correctly identifying lines of symmetry and half-turn symmetry. However, they struggle significantly with focus. Their current learning state shows a strong grasp of math concepts, but they remain highly distracted and disruptive, repeatedly attempting to derail the lesson with off-topic questions (black holes) and explicit prompt injection attempts (demanding the AI ignore instructions and write bad words).
